raw_input:
[4.0/5] Great selection of implements. This is a complete set for doing manicures or pedicures and even a couple for extra things like maybe for your face. All the tools are strong amd sturdy. The case unfortunately is not as strong. The straps that hold the implements in the case are weak and thin and one broke right away. Overall a good set they definitely thought of everything to include in the kit. [5.0/5] Worth every penny This is a fantastic kit. I am really happy with the quality of the materials and the overall aesthetic. Worth it! [4.0/5] It works for Me! This thing has so many things to use that it’s pretty cool to me. It has Nail Clippers that I’ve never seen before but, I think they work really well to get in at angles that I just can’t do with regular ones. I wish the Case that comes with it could be hung up for storage but, I love all of the different tools in it and know that when I feel the next ingrown nail is coming, I will be able to get to it Much easier to avoid it from getting bad.
I would definitely recommend it for the price it is.
Stay Healthy Everyone!😁 [5.0/5] Great value for the price! Now that we are spending more time at home, I wanted to get a manicure kit to treat myself! This set is perfect for what I needed and worth for what I paid. I wasn’t looking for anything fancy, just something that works. I’m happy with all the tools that came and especially surprise to see how compact is this set. It would be great for traveling as well! [5.0/5] Good. Very nice product. It has everything that I need. [5.0/5] Reliable Tools This case, and all the tools inside are very sturdy and reliable. The sharp parts are sharp, and the moving parts move easily. [3.0/5] Mediocre set This is a mediocre set. It isnt especially bad nor good. The scissor blades don't meet in the middle so they dont cut very well. One set of nail clippers has a weird angle and leaves ridges on the surface of the nail. The other does a better job but for some reason launches the pieces of nail far away - not great for keeping the house tidy. The case doesnt stay shut.

Overall, most of the pieces work they just dont do a great job. I'd have preferred a simpler set with fewer pieces of better quality. [5.0/5] very useful Very sharp, very delicate, a full range, especially pointed nail clippers, small but can cut toenails, I like very much [5.0/5] Perfect mani&pedi and facial set! Has everything you would need. Great product, the tools used to extract blackheads are amazing. Also the tools are very sharp. [5.0/5] Great Set! Trimming my nails is an important part of my hygiene, and this set of tools are really nice! Super sharp, and easy to use. I was really impressed with the variety of tools available. Able to knock out any issue you may have. From ingrown nails to eagle claws. One thing I found extra impressive were the trimmers. Usually, you need to soak your nails prior to trimming. If not, they have a tendency to fly off into space, risking eye damage to anyone nearby. These are so sharp, they just melt through my nail like butter. I was a little surprised because I've never seen that happen before. I suspect that'll change as I use them more and more, but it was still impressive to run across that. This will be my one-stop-shop for my nail care needs. Would work great for men or women! [4.0/5] OK There are a couple complaints about the case, but it's actually one of the better features of this kit, attractive, nicely padded, secure, with a place for everything, though it's a test of dexterity to get some of the tools out of their place. Just keep everything where it belongs and it closes easily with a snap.

The tools are mostly fine. They're adequately sturdy. The scissors are a little thin and flexible for tough cuts, but they're supposed to be for trimming facial hair, for which they're fine.

The fit and finish of a few of the tools isn't the best. A couple of the nail clippers have a little overbite, where one blade doesn't perfectly align with the other, which can make for messier cuts. And a couple of the blades on the callus removers are pitted a little.

On the whole, it's an adequate kit for nail and foot care and grooming.

Made in China. [5.0/5] Best callus removal set This callus set is one of the best I’ve had it’s tools are sturdy n sting for when I need a little extra power it’s grinder file cuts deep in the callus without breaking skin or getting dull [5.0/5] Great product! All of the tools come in handy one way or another. They are sharp & sturdy, love it! [4.0/5] Implements galore Fairly comprehensive 18 piece home manicure set with implements for finger and toenails, eye brow grooming, callus treatment, nose hair scissors and an ear wax pick (common for use in Asia). The pieces are all constructed from black painted stainless steel and are mostly machined nicely. The pieces are all set in place in an included, snap shut, carry case. [4.0/5] Has the tools needed to handle our face/hands/feet/skin issues. Short summary: I like a number of the instruments available in this set; they work well for my needs, and for the needs of our family members.
The case securely holds each instrument in its slot; but some instruments are tough to remove from their slots.

More details:

a) Case is soft to the touch and comfortable to hold. There is a tab that I press in to open the case; to close the case, I press both parts of the case together.

b) One compartment (the side with the tab) holds:
--- Nail File
Works well to smooth out my nails after I trimmed them.
--- Fingernail Clipper
The curved blades cut my nails well. The lever has square-shaped divots that give good
grip. It is a bit hard to rotate the lever around the pin; but, with continued use, it should
be easier to open and close the lever. There is no nail file attached to the clipper
--- Nose trimmer
I did not find this to be as good at cutting my nose hairs.
---.Cuticle nipper
This trims my cuticles well.
--- Eyebrow trimmer
This does a decent job in trimming eyebrow
--- Cuspidal/Obtuse push-type broach
I didn't have a need to use this; but several family members used this, and they say it
does an adequate job with their nail polish routine
--- Ear pick
I tried it, but I didn't like the sensation of it scraping against the inside of my ear.

c) The other compartment holds:
--- Toenail clipper
Curved blades cut my toenails well; however, it does not cut my big toenail because
that nail has too much fungus and is too thick. I used a different toenail clipper
recommended by my foot doctor.
--- Oblique nail clipper
I don't use this often for cutting my nail; but I like how easily it cuts the nails. Other
family members love it, and are more familiar in using it
--- Nail cleaning knife
I don't use this; others in my family say it works well to remove the debris under their nails.
--- Tweezers
My family members do not like this, because it doesn't open enough for them to use; so they went
back to using their preferred tweezers
--- Zig Zag callus remover
--- Oblique callus remover
--- Flat callus remover
note: so far, those of us with calluses (including me) do not have them so thick that
they need removal using these instruments.
--- Dead skin fork
a family member used it; she says it is quite useful in removing the loose skin right
around her cuticle area
--- Ear pick (has something like a serrated shape, perhaps)
I much prefer this ear pick; it felt more natural and is like what I used in the past.
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a pick)
--- Acne tool (one end with a circular loop; other end with a scoop)
None of our family members currently have acne, so we haven't had a chance to try it;
but, we have similar instruments just like these, and those worked well when we've
needed to deal with our skin issues.

Lastly: I would have liked the case to be slightly larger, so that it is easier to remove all these instruments. When I removed all the instruments, some of which I used, others which my family members used, and then put the instruments back into the case, we couldn't get it to close. Finally, we removed the toenail clippers, and that easily closed the case.

Overall, this is a good manicure set, with instruments that I and/or my family members find to be very useful for our particular needs. [5.0/5] Way better than expected Half of this stuff looks cool, but I don't even know what its used for...but I love it! [5.0/5] Multi-purpose tools Totally worth it for the price [5.0/5] Excellent Excellent

response:
Most buyers happy with kit. Good value, many tools, sharp and sturdy. Good for nails, cuticles, callus, eyebrows, nose hair, ear, acne, face. Compact, good for travel.

Case weak point - straps thin, one broke, some say case not stay shut, hard remove tools from slots. Few tools have alignment issues, blades not meet perfectly, leave ridges. Scissors little thin. Some nail clippings fly far.

Overall most recommend. Good for whole family. Many tools, good price.
